address.addrLine: The John Rylands Research Institute and Library

addrLine.ref: The John Rylands Research Institute and Library

msIdentifier.repository: The John Rylands Research Institute and Library

msDesc.head: The first of two albums, with Indian Drawings 21, of disparate South Asian paintings, portraits, and calligraphy specimens.

msContents.summary: This album contains primarily thirty-five paintings of primarily South Asian subjects together with a few specimens of Arabic and Persian calligraphy. Many folios bear the seal impression of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809), who served as Chief Justice in Calcutta. Acquired by former owner Alexander Lindsay as a parcel of sixty-two folios, he subsequently selected, arranged, and had them bound in two volumes, together with Indian Drawings 21.

msItem.title: Album of Primarily South Asian Painting and Persian Calligraphy
Rustam engaged in battle
msItem.note: Landscape, rotated 90º counter-clockwise. Bears cut and pasted gold embossed applied borders.

msItem.title: Elephant combat
msItem.note: Landscape, rotated 90º counter-clockwise. The applied black margins recall other album mounts now in the Bodleian.

msItem.title: Hunting Scene
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically. The applied bright red margins recall other album mounts now in the Bodleian.

msItem.title: Couple Hunting
msItem.note: Landscape, rotated 90º counter-clockwise. Pencilled inscription.

msItem.title: A Mughal prince hunts on horseback
msItem.note: Landscape, rotated 90º counter-clockwise. Pencilled inscription.

msItem.title: An Indian woman waters a plant
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.

msItem.title: Angels visit an ascetic
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically. A very finely rendered scene, clearly inspired by Christian imagery.

msItem.title: A group of men appear overcome by a poetry recital
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically. A very finely rendered scene.

msItem.title: Āsādarī, a female ascetic, holds a cobra
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.

msItem.title: Scene from the Rāmāyaṇa
msItem.decoNote: Rāma and Sītā enthroned receive Hanumān
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically. The first of five depictions of Hindu deities and mythical creatures by the same painter.

msItem.title: Vishnu rides Garuda while his multiple arms bear various weapons
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically. The second of five depictions of Hindu deities by the same painter. An English note on the reverse misidentifies the portrait as Seneacher who answers to the god Saturn the English of the above being Saturn day or Saturday- worshipped by the Indoos.

msItem.title: Portrait of Ganga
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically. The third of five depictions of Hindu deities and mythical creatures by the same painter. A note in English on the reverse identifies the subject as Ram Gunga, the Goddess of the Ganges, worshipped by the Indoos.

msItem.title: Portrait of Chinnamastā
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically. The fourth of five depictions of Hindu deities and mythical creatures by the same painter. A note in English on the reverse identifies the subject as Ganganese, the Goddes of revenege, worshipped by the Indoos.

msItem.title: A deer-headed dīv leads a composite horse
msItem.note: Landscape, rotated 90º counter-clockwise. The fifth of five depictions of Hindu deities and mythical creatures by the same painter. An English note on the reverse describes it as Sureege ke Gouna a symbolic representation of the Sun and the 12 signs of the zodiac as worshipped by the Indoos.

msItem.title: Portrait of Gangajī
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically. Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse.

msItem.title: Kahan Jiv Bish Rup
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically. Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse.

msItem.title: Krishna plays flute for Radha
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse.

msItem.title: Krishna plays flute for Radha
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse.

msItem.title: Krishna plays flute at Ras Mandal
msItem.note: Landscape format, rotated 90º counter-clockwise.
Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse.

msItem.title: Krishna plays flute against a tall tree
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse.

msItem.title: Rag Pahrū
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse.

msItem.title: Rama enthroned
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse.

msItem.title: Muẓaffar Dil Khān Bahādur
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse.

msItem.title: Nawwāb Ghār al-Dīn Khān
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse.. Wordham?

msItem.title: Chained elephant and mahout
msItem.note: Landscape format, rotated 90º counter-clockwise.
Four applied borders. Numbered 11 on reverse.

msItem.title: study of a gemstone carved with elephants in combat.
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Tiny jewel study.Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse.

msItem.title: Deccani nobleman
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Applied embossed silver borders. Impey seal.

msItem.title: Quote of
msItem.author: Zamakhshari
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
eal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809).

msItem.title: Mughal scholar and prince
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Handle with caution. Upper surface delaminating from mount.

msItem.title: Mughal ruler- Babur?
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.

msItem.title: Calligraphy by ‘Abd al-Rahm Haravi 1009 AH
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Impey seal. Gold-flecked mount appears similar to 11a.

msItem.title: A seated Princess smokes a hookah, while her attendants watch
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Two small seals on reverse, one illegible, possibly a talisman as it's backwards? 9 x 11. sencond of Muzaffar Khan Bahadur 12 x 14.

msItem.title: Portrait of Nādir Shāh
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse. Also possibly signed Sir J. Sykes(?).

msItem.title: Warrior slays a rhinocerous
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Cut and applied mettalic embossed borders.

msItem.title: Warrior bids farewell
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.

msItem.title: Prince and princess hawking encounter maidens by a river
msItem.note: Landscape format, rotated 90º counter-clockwise.

msItem.title: Prince and princess by an outdoor pool
msItem.note: Portrait format, mounted vertically.
Seal of Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) impressed upon the reverse.

msItem.textLang: Primarily paintings with no linguistic content, together with several in Persian and Hindustani

supportDesc.foliation: Pencilled Arabic numerals inscribed on the lower-right corners of most a sides.
supportDesc.collation: Undetermined due to oversewing. No catchwords
supportDesc.condition: Handle folios with caution: Beware of extensive tears to the interleaving, especially at head and tail. Some areas chipped and abraded at the edges.

binding.p:
Uniformly bound in Britain together with Indian Drawings 21 for former owner
Alexander Lindsay, 25th Earl of Crawford (1812–1880).

Individual folios attached with white cambric strips, with thin white wove interleaving, and compensation guards of camrbic in the gutters laminated with heavy-weight, heavily flocked, European machine-manufactured blue paper, with the same added as endpapers to the beginning and end. Oversewn on five recessed cords laced into pasteboards. Edges untrimmed, with white and black front-bead decorative endbands sewn at head and tail. Covered in half medium brown goastkin leather, hollow-backed, with spattered decorated paper sides. without a flap (Type III binding per Déroche).
binding.decoNote:
Spine pan elas palleted with thick single filllet lines in blind with thin lines in gold on either side. Lettered:
INDIAN
DRAWINGS
in gold with a small paper label underneath numbered Nº 21>.
binding.condition: Handle binding with caution. In fair but stable condition with extensive abrasion and grain layer delamination of the exterior leather.

seal.p:
Rectangular seal impressions, intaglio carved in nasta‘līq script in two stacked lines, double-ruled, of former owner Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809), dated 1775, along with a regnal year 16 of George III, King of Great Britain (b. 1738, r. 1760–1820) reads from bottom upwards:
سر ایلاجه ایمپی چیف جستس سنه ۱۶، ۱۷۷۵
(Sir Īlāhah Īmpī Chīf Justis, sanah 16, 1775).
Dimensions:
19 26

history.origin: The folios originate from different sources, with a majority probbly detached from an album assembled for, or acquired by Sir Elijah Impey (1732–1809) in Calcutta (Kolkata) between 1774 to 1783, as per his seal impression on 3b. Impey served as the first Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of Judicature at Fort William, where he amassed a considerable collection, before he returned to Britain and passed away.

provenance.p: London firm of Harry Phillips (d. 1840) sold Impey's collection 21 May 1810; however, others in India also acquired albums and folios from him both prior to—and possibly after—his return to Britain, so whether this volume sold at that sale, possibly separated from a larger one, remains unclear.
Evidently later obtained by scholar Nathaniel Bland (1803–1865) for his library at Randalls Park, Leatherhead.

After Bland's death, London bookseller Bernard Quaritch (1819–1899) sold his oriental manuscripts to Alexander Lindsay, 25th Earl of Crawford (1812–1880) in June, 1866, paid in two instalments of £450 and £400, and then moved to Bibliotheca Lindesiana at Haigh Hall, Wigan. He likely selected and arranged the contents of the parcel and had them bound in two volumes, together with Indian Drawings 18.
Purchased by Enriqueta Rylands (1843–1908) in 1901 from James Ludovic Lindsay, 26th Earl of Crawford (1847–1913).

acquisition.p: Bequeathed by Enriqueta Rylands (1843–1908) in 1908 to the John Rylands Library, Manchester.

source.p: Bibliographical description based on a manuscript catalogue by Michael Kerney, circa 1890s.
Manuscript description by Jake Benson in 2026 with reference to the volume in hand.

availability.p: To book an in-person or online appointment to consult the manuscript, visit Using the Special Collections Reading Rooms. For any other enquiries please email uml.special-collections@manchester.ac.uk.

listBibl.bibl: F. Weis, ed. Eighteenth-Century Indian Muraqqaʿs: Audiences, Artists, Patrons and Collectors. Leiden: Brill, 2025.

revisionDesc.change: Jake Benson created preliminary record derived from Indian Drawings 21.
